case_name: RXP v East Lancashire Hospitals NHS Trust (Clinical Negligence) [2022] EWHC 133 (QB) (24 January 2022)

summary.brief: Medical malpractice case involving obstetrics gynaecology.

evidence_index.text: The claim arises out of the neurological injuries suffered by the Claimant at the time of his birth, at the Royal Blackburn Hospital, on 3 November 2008.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: The Claimant suffered a hypoxic ischaemic injury at birth which has caused him both physical and cognitive disability.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: He is independently mobile, but he has an abnormal gait and balance difficulties.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: The issues of breach of duty and causation were compromised on terms, approved by Lambert J on 31 July 2019, that the Defendant pay 95% of damages to be assessed.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: The Defendant will pay a retained sum of £7,125,000, and will also pay periodical payments in respect of care and case management.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: A Deputy has been appointed in this case, and in accordance with the form of Order that the Court is asked to approve, those sums will be paid into a bank account of the Deputy.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: It is further proposed that part of the damages, in the sum of £223,274, should be paid out to the Claimant's parents in repayment of expenses incurred by them.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: I have given careful consideration to that advice, together with the other papers in the case to which I have been referred and the assistance with which I have been provided by Counsel this morning.
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's analysis

evidence_index.text: I am therefore happy to give my approval to the settlement and an Order in the form proposed can be made.
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's conclusion

evidence_index.text: The extent to which the family has kept the Claimant so well and looked after him with such care and devotion is apparent not only from their witness statements.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

initial_state.history_of_present_illness: The Claimant suffered a hypoxic ischaemic injury at birth.

requestables.name: Neurology Consultation
requestables.description: Assessment of neurological status and management plan

timeline_phases.name: Birth and Initial Assessment
timeline_phases.description: Newborn presents with neurological injury at birth

decision_points.clinical_context: Newborn with suspected hypoxic ischaemic injury
decision_points.prompt: What immediate management should be initiated?

options.clinical_reasoning: Standard care for hypoxic ischaemic encephalopathy
Supportive care alone may not address the underlying injury

expected_action_court.description: Therapeutic hypothermia should have been initiated

explanation.what_should_have_happened: Therapeutic hypothermia should have been initiated

patient_outcome.description: Claimant suffers from physical and cognitive disabilities
patient_outcome.long_term_effects: Abnormal gait, balance difficulties, neurocognitive impairments

key_findings: Breach of duty and causation were compromised

malpractice_determination.counterfactual: Appropriate management could have mitigated injury

factual_timeline.event: Claimant born at Royal Blackburn Hospital

court_findings.legal_significance: Compromise on breach of duty and causation
